summary: The actual mockup is still a framed screenshot of the expected homepage plus an extra context header, so it preserves the visual reference but does not yet provide editable page structure.

majorDiffAreas.title: Visible page is still a screenshot-backed reference
majorDiffAreas.expected: An editable homepage mockup with DOM sections for navigation, hero, book identity, learning-lab panels, resource rail, and proof band.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The mockup contains a context header and a single source-reference section with the selected generated image embedded as an img.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The target cannot be tuned as real HTML/CSS until the screenshot is replaced by editable modules using verified assets and real content.

evidence.observation: The visible reference body is a header plus section[data-limner-region='source-reference'] containing img src='./source-reference.png'.
The actual screenshot shows the ideal page inset below a separate target context header rather than rebuilt as the whole browser viewport.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Replace the context-header and source-frame scaffold with real editable DOM for the homepage: top nav, hero title/CTAs, book cover asset, learning visualization panels, resource rail, university proof strip, and accolade cards.

majorDiffAreas.title: Extra wrapper chrome changes the first-viewport proportions
majorDiffAreas.expected: The homepage design fills the viewport with the nav, hero, resource rail, and proof band directly in the page flow.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The page adds an outer scaffold header, border, padding, and screenshot frame, shrinking the homepage concept and delaying the proof band visually.
majorDiffAreas.impact: Even before editability, the screenshot is not occupying the same composition or attention flow as the canonical reference.

evidence.observation: Visible text inventory includes only the scaffold header text and 'Selected generated reference image', not the actual homepage content as editable text.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Remove the scaffold header and make the editable homepage itself the root viewport, using the generated image only as off-page guidance.

matches: The selected source image is present and scaled clearly enough for visual reference.
The mockup already uses the intended warm paper, navy, gold, and academic blue palette direction.
The visual reference is correctly associated with the homepage target.
The first pass avoided inventing production assets beyond the generated reference.
uncertainties: Small quote text and exact source-site wording should be constrained to verified local or current-site assets in later implementation.
possibleIntentionalDeviations: The screenshot-backed shell appears intentional as a first-pass reference scaffold, but it should now be replaced by editable DOM.

inputQuality: The comparison artifacts are sufficient to identify the scaffold-vs-editable-page gap.

nextIteration.focus: Convert the screenshot-backed homepage reference into editable HTML/CSS modules using verified local assets and real resource/proof content.
steps: Replace the scaffold header and source image section with an editable nav, hero, visual lab, resource rail, and proof band.
Use public/book-cover-2nd.png, local widget GIFs, local university logos, and proof portraits instead of generated marks.
Update target regions and tokens so Limner can inspect the new DOM sections.
avoidForNow: Do not use the generated reference image as a production page asset.
Do not invent fake university logos or endorsements.
nextIteration.expectedImprovement: The next capture should expose real homepage text and component geometry, enabling meaningful pixel and structure comparison against the ideal image.

rationale: The current actual screenshot contains the source image, but the editable surface is almost empty. The highest-value fix is to build the real HTML/CSS composition so subsequent Limner loops can tune layout, typography, and component fidelity.
